title: Drip vs Constant Contact (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Drip and Constant Contact email marketing tools. See pricing, features, and which platform suits your business best.
h1: Drip vs Constant Contact: Complete Comparison
intro: Both Drip and Constant Contact are established email marketing platforms, but they serve different business needs. Drip specializes in e-commerce automation with deep store integration, while Constant Contact offers broader marketing tools for local businesses and nonprofits. Understanding their strengths will help you choose the right platform for your goals.
choose_a_if: You run an e-commerce store and need advanced revenue tracking and attribution
You want sophisticated automation workflows with a visual builder interface
You prioritize user satisfaction and higher customer ratings
choose_b_if: You're budget-conscious and looking for the lowest entry price
You're a nonprofit or local business needing event management tools
You want to manage social media ad campaigns alongside email marketing

faq.q: Is Drip better than Constant Contact?
faq.a: It depends on your needs. Drip is superior for e-commerce businesses requiring advanced tracking and automation, offering higher user satisfaction ratings. However, Constant Contact is better suited for nonprofits and local businesses needing event management and social media tools at a lower price point.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Drip or Constant Contact?
faq.a: Constant Contact is significantly cheaper, starting at $12/month compared to Drip's $39/month. This makes Constant Contact more accessible for budget-conscious businesses, though you may outgrow its features as your e-commerce needs become more complex.

faq.q: Can I switch from Constant Contact to Drip?
faq.a: Yes, you can export your contact list from Constant Contact and import it into Drip. However, you'll need to manually recreate any automations or campaigns, as the workflow structures differ between platforms. Plan your migration during a low-activity period to minimize disruption.
